question.text: The RBI-SEBI Standing Technical Committee on Exchange Traded Currency and Interest Rate Derivatives was constituted following a joint meeting of RBI and SEBI. On which date was this joint meeting held?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The text states: "it was decided in a joint meeting of RBI and SEBI on February 28, 2008, that an RBI-SEBI Standing Technical Committee on Exchange Traded Currency and Interest Rate Derivatives would be constituted." **The Trap:** April 20, 2007 is when RBI issued comprehensive guidelines on OTC foreign exchange derivatives. May 29, 2008 is when the Committee submitted its report on Currency Futures. August 6, 2008 is when Currency Futures (Reserve Bank) Directions came into effect. **Cross-Exam Context:** Key dates sequence: April 2007 (RBI OTC guidelines) → April 2008 (Internal Working Group report) → February 28, 2008 (joint meeting) → May 29, 2008 (Committee report) → August 6, 2008 (Directions effective). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** February 28, 2008 marks the formal birth of the RBI-SEBI coordination mechanism for currency derivatives.
